anchor.description: x-ui-surface=internal:math-resolver ‚Äî snake_case identifier unique within the entry (e.g., "api_usd_per_1m_tokens_sonnet_input").

description.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors ‚Äî one-line human description of what this prior represents.

kb_source.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî slug of the Atlas entry that owns this citation (e.g., "anthropic", "shopify").

source_url.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî canonical source URL. HTTPS only; must resolve under the ingest domain allowlist (enforced at fetch time).

source_tier.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî tier per SOURCES.md taxonomy.

publish_date.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî publication date of the cited source (not fetch date). Drives staleness gate.

retrieved_at.description: x-ui-surface=internal:provenance-hash ‚Äî ISO-8601 timestamp of the fetch that produced sha256.

sha256.description: x-ui-surface=internal:provenance-hash ‚Äî SHA-256 (hex) of the fetched source body. Re-compute on refetch; mismatch flags the entry for review.

bytes_integrity.description: x-ui-surface=internal:provenance-hash ‚Äî tag describing what `sha256` actually hashes. `clean` = sha matches article bytes. `captcha_wall` / `paywall` / `cdn_geoblock` = sha covers a wall, content retrieved via WebFetch. `webfetch_only_no_raw_html` = SPA shell / JS-rendered page where raw HTML carries no article body (e.g., stripe.com/blog SPA); content extracted via WebFetch only. When ‚âÝ `clean`, `content_sha256` is REQUIRED.

content_sha256.description: x-ui-surface=internal:provenance-hash ‚Äî SHA-256 of extracted MD body. Stable across captcha/paywall variants. REQUIRED when `bytes_integrity` ‚âÝ `clean`; when `bytes_integrity` = `clean`, optional.

is_ic.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî TRUE iff the speaker/author is an individual contributor (not exec, VP, marketing, or vendor partner). REQUIRED true for E_conference to qualify as a prior source per plan ¬ß6.3 ("E is B-equivalent only when speaker is IC"). Default undefined; refinement treats undefined as false for tier E.

anchor.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî optional heading/section anchor within the source (e.g., "¬ß6.1 Availability").

corroborated_by.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî secondary citations. For NON-PRIOR quant claims only (e.g., revenue on AI-private where dual-C is permitted per plan ¬ß6.3). Does NOT rescue tier C/D for math priors ‚Äî priors still require B/E/G per `citationIsPriorAcceptable`.

citation.description: x-ui-surface=section:Entry > Sources ‚Äî provenance record. Every numeric prior carries one.

confidence.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors ‚Äî [0,1] ‚Äî reviewer-assigned confidence in this prior.

verification_status.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors ‚Äî verification state (AI-private entries commonly "partial").

units.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors > Throughput ‚Äî rate unit. Request-rate (rps/rpm/rph/qps), data-rate (bytes_per_second .. pb_per_day), or message/event-rate (messages_per_second, events_per_second). Choose the unit that keeps `value` in a human-reasonable range.

measurement.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors > Throughput ‚Äî what this rate represents (peak burst, sustained average, or tail window).

window.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors > Throughput ‚Äî optional window label (e.g., "1s", "5m", "BFCM 2025").

concurrency.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors > Throughput ‚Äî typical concurrent clients/connections producing this rate.

replicas.description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors > Throughput ‚Äî backend fleet size at the time of measurement.

description: x-ui-surface=section:Math Priors > Throughput ‚Äî scalar throughput observation (peak/sustained/tail).
title: ThroughputPrior
